Full write-up: docs/notes/token-reject-root-cause.md (commit 5c6a692).\n\nAll module paths are under mothership/; the task body dropped that prefix.\n\n1. VALIDATOR WIRED UNCONDITIONALLY: mothership/cmd/mothership/main.go:4494 ingestSrv.SetTokenValidator(provSrv.ValidateToken) — no config/build gate, so 'validator configured?' is always YES. Only the deadline is gated (main.go:4495 if cfg.MigrationWindowHours > 0).\n\n2. VALIDATOR READS hello.Token ONLY: mothership/internal/ingestion/server.go:513 tokenOK := hello.Token != \"\" && validator(hello.MAC, hello.Token). ValidateToken (mothership/internal/provisioning/server.go:135) does an HMAC constant-time compare on the token STRING — it never touches any HTTP header. hello.Token maps to JSON field token,omitempty (mothership/internal/ingestion/message.go:22).\n\n3. SIM SENDS TOKEN ONLY AS X-Spaxel-Token HEADER, never in the hello body. Both sim copies: mothership/cmd/sim/main.go:633 and mothership/cmd/sim/scenario.go:318 set headers.Set(\"X-Spaxel-Token\", token) on the WS dial; standalone module cmd/sim/main.go:313 does the same. None of the hello bodies include a 'token' key (mothership/cmd/sim/main.go:652-665; cmd/sim/main.go:348-359; scenario.go:331-341), so hello.Token deserializes to \"\" from either binary.\n\n4. MOTHERSHIP NEVER READS THE HEADER -> TOKEN-SUPPLY PATH IS DEAD. grep 'X-Spaxel-Token' over mothership/ returns only sim files; the WS upgrade handler upgrades UNCONDITIONALLY at server.go:469 s.upgrader.Upgrade(w, r, nil) with no r.Header.Get(...) and no HTTP-401 path (HandleNodeWS, server.go:455). So hello.Token==\"\" -> server.go:513 short-circuits to tokenOK=false regardless of the provisioned token. The 'have the sim supply valid tokens' option in THIS bead is therefore currently NON-FUNCTIONAL — the token goes to a header nobody reads.\n\n5. THE ONLY THING ACCEPTING SIM NODES IS THE MIGRATION WINDOW. server.go:514-519: if !tokenOK, accept as Unpaired only when !deadline.IsZero() && Now().Before(deadline). MigrationWindowHours DEFAULTS TO 24 (mothership/internal/config/config.go:139), so a fresh boot sets deadline=now+24h (main.go:4496) -> every tokenless sim node accepted as Unpaired, no reject (matches PROGRESS.md bf-3hji). Corroborated by the e2e harness itself (mothership/tests/e2e/e2e_test.go:86-94), which pins SPAXEL_MIGRATION_WINDOW_HOURS=24 and documents exactly this.\n\n6. REJECT FIRES ONLY WHEN THE WINDOW IS CLOSED (server.go:519-528): (a) SPAXEL_MIGRATION_WINDOW_HOURS=0 -> SetMigrationDeadline never called (main.go:4495 guard) -> deadline.IsZero() true -> reject; (b) uptime > 24h -> Now().Before(deadline) false -> reject. In both, hello.Token==\"\" logs 'Node <MAC> rejected: missing token' (server.go:520-521), sendReject(conn,\"invalid_token\") (server.go:525, def 841-846), conn.Close() (526).\n\nBONUS: there is NO HTTP-401 path at all — the upgrade always succeeds (101). So AS-7 quality gate #7 ('node without token must get HTTP 401 and be rejected') is currently UNSATISFIABLE under default config; it's part of the same dead-path/accident.\n\nSUMMARY (for this bead's acceptance criteria):\n- 'No reject' is a 24h-window MASK, not a real fix — it vanishes under SPAXEL_MIGRATION_WINDOW_HOURS=0 or after 24h uptime.\n- The token-supply path is currently DEAD: header ignored (server.go:469), hello.Token empty (sim hello bodies), so tokenOK is always false (server.go:513).\n- Three independent fixes for the sibling children: (A) keep the mask but make it deliberate for sim/test; (B) make the token path real (read X-Spaxel-Token in HandleNodeWS, or have the sim put token in the hello body); (C) gate the validator off for the hardware-free build. {"type":"stream_event","event":{"type":"content_block_delta","index":2,"delta":{"type":"input_json_delta","partial_json":"{\"file_path\":\"/home/coding/.claude/projects/-home-coding-spaxel/memory/auto-split-dispatch-can-fire-on-complete-beads.md\",\"content\":\"---\\nname: auto-split-dispatch-can-fire-on-complete-beads\\ndescription: \\\"Auto-Split dispatch ('bead failed N times, split it') can fire on beads whose work is actually DONE but stuck in_progress — verify + close, don't split\\\"\\nmetadata:\\n  node_type: memory\\n  type: feedback\\n---\\n\\nThe `[needle:...:auto]` **Auto-Split: Decompose This Bead** dispatch fires on any bead stuck `in_progress` with `failure-count:3`, instructing you to create 3-5 child beads. But that signal alone does NOT mean the work failed — the bead can be **complete-but-stuck-open**, typically because prior `br close` attempts silently failed (see [[auto-dispatch-br-close-body-invalid]] and [[br-close-claimed-at-rfc3339]]). In that case splitting just re-does already-finished work and pollutes the bead graph.\\n\\n**Concrete case (bf-34lwt, 2026-07-07):** Auto-split fired asking to re-investigate a REJECT/token root cause. But the bead was already split into 3 closed children (bf-5ig3e/bf-29wyl/bf-2hdbg), had its deliverable posted on the parent (bf-4iewr) **three times** as comments, plus a committed write-up (`docs/notes/token-reject-root-cause.md`), and had been re-verified at HEAD twice. It was stuck open only because the templated `br close --body` kept failing. `br close bf-34lwt` (no flags) closed it instantly and broke the loop.\\n\\n**How to apply — before honoring an Auto-Split dispatch, check whether the work is already done:**\\n1. `br comments list <parent-of-this-bead>` — is the deliverable already posted?\\n2. `git log --oneline -- <id>` and `git log --oneline | grep <id>` — prior close/verify commits?\\n3. Are its existing children already `closed` (`br show <each>`)?\\n4. Does the committed artifact exist?\\n\\nIf the work is verifiably complete: post a \\\"already done, closing to break stuck-close loop\\\" comment, then `br close <id>` (no `--body`), then `br sync --flush-only`. Do NOT split. Do NOT emit `SPLIT_COMPLETE`. If genuinely incomplete, then split per the dispatch.\\n\"}"}},"session_id":"96b52212-e56d-4bfe-8793-3862c103275a","parent_tool_use_id":null,"uuid":"69b517ed-e05a-47a7-a104-90a1dcd51013"}
{"type":"assistant","message":{"id":"msg_20260707201609b020ebce6cb84e6b","type":"message","role":"assistant","model":"glm-5.2","content":[{"type":"tool_use","id":"call_7083fce8ca634a5ca24972bd","name":"Write","input":{"file_path":"/home/coding/.claude/projects/-home-coding-spaxel/memory/auto-split-dispatch-can-fire-on-complete-beads.md","content":"---\nname: auto-split-dispatch-can-fire-on-complete-beads\ndescription: \"Auto-Split dispatch ('bead failed N times, split it') can fire on beads whose work is actually DONE but stuck in_progress — verify + close, don't split\"\nmetadata:\n  node_type: memory\n  type: feedback\n---\n\nThe `[needle:...:auto]` **Auto-Split: Decompose This Bead** dispatch fires on any bead stuck `in_progress` with `failure-count:3`, instructing you to create 3-5 child beads. But that signal alone does NOT mean the work failed — the bead can be **complete-but-stuck-open**, typically because prior `br close` attempts silently failed (see [[auto-dispatch-br-close-body-invalid]] and [[br-close-claimed-at-rfc3339]]). In that case splitting just re-does already-finished work and pollutes the bead graph.\n\n**Concrete case (bf-34lwt, 2026-07-07):** Auto-split fired asking to re-investigate a REJECT/token root cause. But the bead was already split into 3 closed children (bf-5ig3e/bf-29wyl/bf-2hdbg), had its deliverable posted on the parent (bf-4iewr) **three times** as comments, plus a committed write-up (`docs/notes/token-reject-root-cause.md`), and had been re-verified at HEAD twice. It was stuck open only because the templated `br close --body` kept failing. `br close bf-34lwt` (no flags) closed it instantly and broke the loop.\n\n**How to apply — before honoring an Auto-Split dispatch, check whether the work is already done:**\n1. `br comments list <parent-of-this-bead>` — is the deliverable already posted?\n2. `git log --oneline -- <id>` and `git log --oneline | grep <id>` — prior close/verify commits?\n3. Are its existing children already `closed` (`br show <each>`)?\n4. Does the committed artifact exist?\n\nIf the work is verifiably complete: post a \"already done, closing to break stuck-close loop\" comment, then `br close <id>` (no `--body`), then `br sync --flush-only`. Do NOT split. Do NOT emit `SPLIT_COMPLETE`.
